acceptance_criteria: `architect` step's declared `output:` schema in plan-level.yaml includes a `research_needs` array matching issue-2's payload shape.
Architect prompt includes guidance on when to emit `research_needs` vs. an open question vs. proceeding.
Architect prompt has a new StrictUndefined-safe re-entry branch that surfaces research findings and the prior plan, instructing surgical refinement.
Existing re-entry branches (revise_counter, extract_parent_patch, open_questions_gate) remain untouched and continue to work.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; lint-jinja-resolver and lint-type-agnostic checks pass.
description:
## Summary
Extend the `architect` step's `output:` schema in [.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml](.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml) to include a `research_needs` array matching the payload shape defined by sibling issue-2. Update [.conductor/registry/prompts/architect-plan-level.md](.conductor/registry/prompts/architect-plan-level.md) to (a) teach the architect when emitting `research_needs` is preferable to raising an open question or guessing, and (b) add a new re-entry branch that fires when the prior step in `context.history` is the research sub-workflow, surfacing the findings document(s) to the architect for incorporation into the next plan iteration.

## Scope
- Output schema field: `research_needs: array` of objects with whatever shape issue-2 locks (topic / context / why-needed at minimum).
- Prompt guidance: a short section explaining the three-way choice — proceed, open_question (gates on human), research_needs (gates on archive/research, no human).
- New StrictUndefined-safe re-entry branch in the prompt template, mirroring the existing `revise_counter` / `extract_parent_patch` / `open_questions_gate` branches, that surfaces findings.
- Prior plan must be passed through to the re-entry branch so the architect refines surgically rather than regenerating.

## Out of Scope
- The workflow wiring that *invokes* the research sub-workflow (task-2).
- The research sub-workflow itself or its findings shape (sibling issue-2).
title: Extend architect output schema + prompt to emit and consume `research_needs`

acceptance_criteria: `architect` step routes to a new `research_dispatch` node when `research_needs` is non-empty AND the `research:` profile block is present, taking precedence over the open_questions route.

After `research_dispatch` returns, control routes back to `architect` and never touches `open_questions_gate`, `ensure_plan_branch`, `write_plan`, `commit_and_push`, `open_plan_pr`, or `plan_reviewer`.
A `research_loop_counter` derived from `context.history` caps repeated `research_needs` emissions; cap default is profile-overridable.
When the research cap is reached, control surfaces a human gate or proceeds to `open_questions_policy` (whichever the design lands on) — no infinite loop is possible.
Repos without a `research:` profile block see no behavioural change in `plan-level.yaml` execution (regression check on existing harness scenarios).
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass; new tests cover the new routing edges and the cap path.
Polyphony's P5 (no hard-coded state names) is honoured — gating is on payload presence + profile config, not on parent-process state vocabulary.
description:
## Summary
In [.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml](.conductor/registry/workflows/plan-level.yaml), change the `architect` step's `routes:` so that when `architect.output.research_needs | length > 0` AND the `research:` profile block is configured, control flows to a new `research_dispatch` node that invokes the research sub-workflow defined by sibling issue-2/3/4. After the sub-workflow returns, route directly back to `architect`. The path must NOT touch `open_questions_policy`, `open_questions_gate`, `ensure_plan_branch`, `write_plan`, `commit_and_push`, `open_plan_pr`, or `plan_reviewer`. Add a `research_loop_counter` script step (mirroring `open_questions_counter` / `revise_counter`) that derives iteration count from `context.history` and caps the loop, surfacing a `research_cap_gate` (or auto-proceeding) when the cap is reached.

## Scope
- New routing edges off `architect` for the research case (precedence: research before open_questions).
- New `research_dispatch` node invoking the research sub-workflow.
- New `research_loop_counter` step + cap policy resolution (default cap profile-overridable; mirror existing counter pattern).
- New `research_cap_gate` (or fall-through to existing flow) when cap is hit.
- Profile gating: when `.polyphony-config/profile.yaml` has no `research:` block, the new edges are unreachable and the workflow behaves identically to today.

## Out of Scope
- The research sub-workflow itself (sibling issue-2) and its agents (issues 3/4).
- The architect prompt + schema changes (task-1).
- The end-to-end proof scenario (task-3).
title: Wire research-loop routing in plan-level.yaml bypassing the plan-review gate

acceptance_criteria: A single end-to-end demonstration (harness scenario OR dogfood recipe, per Q3 resolution) exercises the architect → research → architect loop on a non-trivial prompt.
The demonstration runs without human intervention.
The captured trace (or harness assertions) confirm bypass of `open_questions_gate`, `ensure_plan_branch`, `write_plan`, `open_plan_pr`, and `plan_reviewer`.
If a harness scenario, it runs in CI alongside existing harness scenarios and passes deterministically.
If a dogfood scenario, the recipe is committed under [docs/](docs/) and the captured trace is attached to the parent issue.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass.
description:
## Summary
Produce *one* end-to-end demonstration that the full architect → research → architect loop closes on a non-trivial prompt without human intervention. The acceptance criterion in the parent issue allows either a path-coverage harness scenario under [tests/harness/scenarios/](tests/harness/scenarios/) **or** a live dogfood scenario; the choice is gated on open question Q3.

## Scope (harness path)
- New scenario directory under [tests/harness/scenarios/](tests/harness/scenarios/), modelled on the existing `close_out_happy_path` and `cascade_remedy_no_stale` scenarios.
- FakeProvider script for the architect that emits `research_needs` on its first invocation, then emits a complete plan on its second invocation after receiving findings.
- FakeProvider script for the research sub-workflow's agents that returns a synthesised findings document.
- Assertions that the trace shows architect → research_dispatch → architect, with no traversal of `open_questions_gate`, `ensure_plan_branch`, `write_plan`, `open_plan_pr`, or `plan_reviewer`.

## Scope (dogfood path)
- A reproducible recipe (markdown doc + pinned prompt) that runs polyphony against a live non-trivial work item, captures the run trace, and demonstrates the loop closing.

## Out of Scope
- Any harness/dogfood proof for the broader research subsystem (issues 2/3/4 own their own coverage).
- Multi-iteration deep-research escalation behaviour (issue-3's territory).
title: End-to-end proof scenario for architect → research → architect loop
